title: Callista haemoglossa
taxonomy: Callista haemoglossa is classified within the phylum Mollusca, class Bivalvia, and family Veneridae. This species of clam belongs to the genus Callista, which is a group of marine bivalves. The family Veneridae is a large group of marine clams and mussels that are commonly found in shallow coastal waters worldwide. Callista haemoglossa is a specific species within this classification, characterized by its distinctive shell and habitat preferences. The classification of Callista haemoglossa reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other species within the phylum Mollusca.

intro: Callista haemoglossa, also known as the blood-red callista or blood-red calleta, is a species of sea snail-eating marine bivalve mollusk. The plant, specifically its extract, has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions, such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Callista haemoglossa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the most common herbal preparations made with Callista haemoglossa include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues.
